tasks.title: Initialize Next.js project
tasks.description: Run 'npx create-next-app@latest . --typescript --tailwind --app --no-src-dir --import-alias @/*' in the repo root. Verify package.json, tsconfig.json, tailwind.config.ts, and app/page.tsx are created.
tasks.output: Created a runnable Next.js 16 + TypeScript + Tailwind scaffold inside web/ to keep the repo root for coordination files, installed dependencies, and verified the app builds successfully.

tasks.title: Configure Anthropic API client
tasks.description: Install @anthropic-ai/sdk. Create web/lib/anthropic.ts exporting a singleton Anthropic client. Create web/.env.local with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=placeholder.
tasks.output: Installed the Anthropic SDK in the nested web app, added a shared client helper, and created a local environment placeholder for ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.

tasks.title: Design unified task data schema
tasks.description: Create web/types/schema.ts with the ConsultItem TypeScript interface matching the mockup data model: { id: string; title: string; kind: 'task'|'blocker'|'risk'|'waiting'; status: 'todo'|'doing'|'waiting'|'done'; owner: string; ownerName: string; due: string; dueFlag: 'soon'|'overdue'|null; tags: string[]; waiting: boolean; quote: string|null; suggested_action?: string; suggested_owner?: string }. Also export a Stats interface: { total, waiting, blockers, risks }.
tasks.output: Created web/types/schema.ts exporting ConsultItem, ItemKind, ItemStatus, DueFlag, Stats, ClientDigest, SummaryData, ExtractResponse, and SummarizeResponse interfaces.

tasks.title: Set up dark theme design tokens
tasks.description: Replace web/app/globals.css with dark theme CSS variables from ConsultFlow.html: --bg, --bg-1, --bg-2, --bg-3, --line, --line-2, --fg, --fg-dim, --fg-mute, --fg-faint, --accent, --accent-weak, --accent-glow, --warn, --warn-weak, --danger, --danger-weak, --ok, --ok-weak, --info, --info-weak, --radius, --radius-lg, --pad-y. Import Inter Tight and JetBrains Mono from Google Fonts in web/app/layout.tsx. Add dark scrollbar, shimmer @keyframes, cardIn @keyframes, pulse @keyframes, spin @keyframes to globals.css.
tasks.output: Replaced the global styling with the dark ConsultFlow token system, added animations and dark scrollbars, and switched layout fonts to Inter Tight and JetBrains Mono.

tasks.title: Expose CSS variables as Tailwind utilities
tasks.description: Update web/tailwind.config.ts to add theme.extend.colors mapping CSS variables to Tailwind utilities: bg: 'var(--bg)', bg-1: 'var(--bg-1)', bg-2, bg-3, line: 'var(--line)', fg: 'var(--fg)', fg-dim, fg-mute, fg-faint, accent: 'var(--accent)', accent-weak, warn, danger, ok, info. Add fontFamily for 'inter-tight' and 'mono' (JetBrains Mono).
tasks.output: Mapped the dark theme CSS variables into Tailwind colors, fonts, radius, and animation utilities for the prototype shell.

tasks.title: Build app shell layout
tasks.description: Replace web/app/page.tsx with the top-level shell: 'use client', full-viewport grid (232px sidebar + 1fr main), main column with 44px topbar + workspace row. Workspace is a 3-column grid (360px left | 1fr center | 300px right). Each region is a placeholder div for now. Accept and pass down items state and selectedId state as props/context.
tasks.output: Replaced the landing page with a client-side app shell including sidebar, topbar, and three-column workspace using prototype state for items and selection.

tasks.title: Build Sidebar component
tasks.description: Create web/components/Sidebar.tsx: brand mark (gradient div with ::after arc), 'ConsultFlow' name + green status dot, nav items (Inbox, My Tasks, Waiting on Client, Risks) with icons and counts, Engagements section with color pips and counts, Views section (Board/Timeline/Client digest), user footer with avatar initials. All items use CSS variables for colors.
tasks.output: Built the dark sidebar with branding, nav sections, engagement list, view switcher, and user footer using live prototype counts.

tasks.title: Build Source panel (InputPanel)
tasks.description: Rebuild web/components/InputPanel.tsx to match mockup. Dark theme. Three source tabs (Transcript, Email, Notes) with active underline. Metadata pills row (duration, speakers, date, word count). Scrollable content area showing the raw text. Sticky footer with hint (⌘↵), Clear button, and Re-parse primary button with ring-pulse animation when attn prop is true. Props: { value, onChange, onParse, isParsing, activeTab, onTabChange }. Previous light-theme component must be replaced entirely.
tasks.output: Rebuilt the source panel in the dark mockup style with tabs, metadata pills, scrollable source text, clear action, and animated re-parse button state.

tasks.title: Build Card component
tasks.description: Create web/components/Card.tsx. Card shows: card-top row (mono id + kind badge colored by type: k-task/k-blocker/k-risk/k-waiting + 'from client' badge if waiting), card-title, card-meta row (owner avatar circle with gradient by initials, due date with overdue/soon color, first tag). Apply waiting-highlight border + left accent bar when item.waiting=true. Apply selected ring when selected prop is true. cardIn animation on mount. Props: { item: ConsultItem; selected?: boolean; onClick: () => void }.
tasks.output: Added a reusable schema-typed Card component with kind badges, owner avatar gradients, due-state coloring, waiting-on-client highlighting, and selected-state ring styling for the upcoming board view.

tasks.title: Build Board component (kanban)
tasks.description: Create web/components/Board.tsx. Four columns: Todo, In progress, Waiting on client, Done. Each column has a col-head (colored swatch dot, label, count, + add icon) and scrollable col-body rendering Card components. Filter bar above columns with filter chips (All types, Any owner, This sprint) and Group label. During skeleton mode, render skel cards with shimmer animation. Props: { items: ConsultItem[]; selectedId: string|null; onSelect: (id:string)=>void; loading?: boolean }.
tasks.output: Added the kanban Board component with filter chips, four status columns, skeleton loading cards, empty states, and Card-based rendering wired for item selection.

tasks.title: Build LoadingOverlay component
tasks.description: Create web/components/LoadingOverlay.tsx. Fullscreen backdrop (blur + semi-transparent). Centered loading-card with title + spinner. Four loading-step rows: 'Segmenting by speaker', 'Identifying commitments & owners', 'Classifying blockers, risks, waiting-on-client', 'Linking to source quotes'. Steps animate through: completed steps show ✓ in ok color, current step shows mini spinner, future steps are dimmed. Props: { visible: boolean; currentStep: number }.
tasks.output: Added a fullscreen loading overlay component with blurred backdrop, animated spinner card, and four staged extraction steps that visually distinguish completed, active, and upcoming work.

tasks.title: Build RightPanel component
tasks.description: Rebuild web/components/RightPanel.tsx to match mockup. Delete web/components/SummaryPanel.tsx (orphaned old component) at the same time. Two modes controlled by selectedItem prop: (1) Summary mode (selectedItem=null): panel-head 'Executive summary' + copy/regenerate icons, stat grid (Action items/Waiting/Blockers/Risks), progress bar (done/doing/waiting/todo segments), exec summary paragraphs, client digest items, provenance footer. (2) Detail mode (selectedItem set): panel-head showing item id, kind badges + status badge, detail-title, detail-grid (Owner/Due/Status/Type/Tags/Engagement), source quote block, 'Add to digest' + 'Back' buttons. Props: { items: ConsultItem[]; summary: SummaryData|null; selectedItem: ConsultItem|null; onBack: ()=>void }.
tasks.output: Replaced the old summary panel with a new RightPanel supporting executive-summary and item-detail modes, derived stats/progress, digest/provenance sections, copy action, and detail metadata with source quote handling.

tasks.title: Write extraction prompt
tasks.description: Write web/prompts/extract.txt. System prompt instructing the LLM to extract all action items, blockers, risks, and client-waiting items from raw transcript/email/notes text. For each item output JSON matching ConsultItem schema: id (sequential e.g. ACM-101), title, kind (task|blocker|risk|waiting), status (todo|doing|waiting|done — use 'waiting' for items blocked on client), owner (2-3 letter initials), ownerName (full name + '(client)' if external), due (natural language), dueFlag (soon|overdue|null), tags (2-3 lowercase topic tags), waiting (true if client must act), quote (verbatim excerpt from source, max 30 words). Return only a JSON array.
tasks.output: Written at web/prompts/extract.txt. Includes field-by-field schema instructions, kind/status classification rules, quote extraction guidance, and a complete example output object.

tasks.title: Implement POST /api/extract via Extractor Agent
tasks.description: Create web/app/api/extract/route.ts. Use the Managed Agents pattern: (1) Read EXTRACTOR_AGENT_ID from process.env (set by TASK-023 setup script). (2) Create a session: anthropic.beta.agents.sessions.create({ agent_id: EXTRACTOR_AGENT_ID }). (3) Send the user's input text as a user message in the session. (4) Poll/stream until session completes and extract the assistant's text response. (5) JSON.parse the response into ConsultItem[]. (6) Compute stats { total, waiting, blockers, risks }. (7) Return { items, stats }. Handle missing agent ID (500) and JSON parse errors (422). Beta header required: 'managed-agents-2026-04-01'.
tasks.output: Added the extract API route backed by managed-agent sessions, including environment-aware session startup, streamed response collection, stats computation, and 422 handling for invalid JSON output.

tasks.title: Write executive summary and client digest prompt
tasks.description: Write web/prompts/summarize.txt. System prompt that takes a JSON array of ConsultItems and returns a JSON object: { execSummary: string (2-3 HTML paragraphs using <span class='em'>, <span class='em-warn'>, <span class='em-risk'> for emphasis), clientDigest: { nextFromClient: string, nextFromUs: string, flags: string }, provenance: string }. Executive summary should call out overall status, client-gating items, and risks. Client digest should be concise bullet-style sentences.
tasks.output: Written at web/prompts/summarize.txt. Instructs the model to produce execSummary as styled HTML paragraphs with em/em-warn/em-risk spans, a three-field clientDigest object, and a provenance string. Includes a complete example JSON output.

tasks.title: Implement POST /api/summarize via Execution Agent
tasks.description: Create web/app/api/summarize/route.ts. Use the Managed Agents pattern: (1) Read EXECUTION_AGENT_ID from process.env (set by TASK-023 setup script). (2) Create a session: anthropic.beta.agents.sessions.create({ agent_id: EXECUTION_AGENT_ID }). (3) Send JSON.stringify(items) as the user message. (4) Poll/stream until session completes and extract the assistant's response text. (5) JSON.parse into { execSummary, clientDigest, provenance }. (6) Return the parsed object. Handle missing agent ID (500) and JSON parse errors (422). Beta header required: 'managed-agents-2026-04-01'.
tasks.output: Added the summarize API route backed by managed-agent sessions, sharing streamed response handling and returning parsed summary JSON with structured route errors.

tasks.title: Integrate parse flow into page.tsx
tasks.description:
NOTE: page.tsx was accidentally reverted to the old prototype shell. Redo the full integration from scratch.

Replace web/app/page.tsx with the complete integrated shell:
1. Per-tab source text: replace single sourceText string with Record<SourceTab, string> (keys: Transcript/Email/Notes, initial value only on Transcript). Pass sourceText[activeTab] as value and update only the active key in onChange.
2. Parse flow: Re-parse button triggers POST /api/extract with input: sourceText[activeTab]. Show LoadingOverlay with 4 animated steps over ~1.5s. On success update items and stats state.
3. Render real Board and RightPanel components (not placeholders).
4. Use useCallback and useState for items, stats, loading, selectedId, summary state.
tasks.output: Replaced the reverted prototype page with the integrated shell: per-tab source text state, extract API parsing flow with the staged loading overlay, live items/stats updates, and real Board and RightPanel rendering wired into page state.

tasks.title: Integrate summary flow
tasks.description: After extraction succeeds in page.tsx, automatically POST items to /api/summarize and store the result in summary state. Pass summary + stats to RightPanel so the Executive Summary view populates with real AI-generated content including exec summary paragraphs, client digest, and provenance. Add a Regenerate button (in RightPanel panel-head) that re-calls /api/summarize on demand.
tasks.output: Extended page.tsx to automatically call /api/summarize after successful extraction, store the returned executive summary and client digest in page state, surface summary failures separately from extraction failures, and wire the RightPanel regenerate control to rerun summarization on demand.

tasks.title: Wire card selection to RightPanel detail view
tasks.description: In page.tsx, wire Board's onSelect callback to update selectedId state. Pass the selected ConsultItem to RightPanel. RightPanel switches to detail mode showing item metadata and source quote. 'Back' button clears selectedId, returning to summary view. Clicking a highlighted span in InputPanel's transcript text should also open the corresponding card detail.
tasks.output: Wired page-level selection so board card clicks and transcript quote highlights both set selectedId, the matching ConsultItem is passed into RightPanel detail mode, and the Back action clears the selection to return to the executive summary view.

tasks.title: Polish end-to-end demo experience
tasks.description: Final pass: (1) Topbar breadcrumbs showing engagement + session name, view tabs (Board/List/Timeline) with active state. (2) ⌘↵ keyboard shortcut triggers parse. (3) Highlight extracted quotes as colored spans in InputPanel transcript (task=accent, blocker=warn, risk=danger, waiting=info). Clicking a span opens the card detail. (4) Tweaks panel (accent color swatches, density toggle, replay parse button). (5) 'Share digest' button copies client digest to clipboard. (6) Empty states for Board and RightPanel when no items exist.
tasks.output: Polished the demo flow with breadcrumb/session topbar treatment, active view tabs, page-level tweaks controls for accent and density plus replay parse, explicit board and summary empty states, and a clearer Share digest control that copies the generated client digest.

tasks.title: Write agent system prompts (claude-owned)
tasks.description:
Write the three Managed Agent system prompt files. Each file is the system prompt that will be stored on the Agent object at agents.create() time — it does NOT go in the session message.

- web/prompts/agent-extractor.txt: Extractor Agent. Extracts ConsultItems from raw text and returns a JSON array matching the ConsultItem schema.
- web/prompts/agent-execution.txt: Execution Agent. Takes a JSON array of ConsultItems and returns { execSummary (HTML paragraphs with em/em-warn/em-risk spans), clientDigest { nextFromClient, nextFromUs, flags }, provenance }.
- web/prompts/agent-email.txt: Email Agent. Takes { items: ConsultItem[], summary: SummaryData } and returns { subject, body, tone } for a client-facing update email.

Prompts are already written. This task is done.
tasks.output: Written three Managed Agent system prompts: agent-extractor.txt (ConsultItem extraction with full field schema and classification rules), agent-execution.txt (executive summary + client digest with HTML em spans), agent-email.txt (client-facing update email with subject/body/tone).

tasks.title: Create managed agents setup script
tasks.description:
Create web/scripts/setup-agents.ts. This script runs once to create the three persistent Managed Agent objects and prints their IDs for .env.local.

Steps:
1. Import Anthropic SDK with beta header 'managed-agents-2026-04-01'.
2. Read each system prompt file from web/prompts/agent-extractor.txt, agent-execution.txt, agent-email.txt using fs.readFileSync.
3. Call anthropic.beta.agents.create() for each:
   - Extractor: { name: 'ConsultFlow Extractor', model: 'claude-haiku-4-5', system: <extractor prompt> }
   - Execution: { name: 'ConsultFlow Execution', model: 'claude-haiku-4-5', system: <execution prompt> }
   - Email: { name: 'ConsultFlow Email', model: 'claude-haiku-4-5', system: <email prompt> }
4. Print the three agent IDs to stdout in .env format:
   EXTRACTOR_AGENT_ID=<id>
   EXECUTION_AGENT_ID=<id>
   EMAIL_AGENT_ID=<id>
5. Append these to web/.env.local automatically.

Add a package.json script: 'setup-agents': 'npx tsx scripts/setup-agents.ts'

IMPORTANT: agents.create() is called only once. Subsequent calls create new agents (waste money). The script should check if IDs already exist in .env.local and skip re-creation.
tasks.output: Created a setup-agents script that provisions a managed environment plus extractor/execution/email agents only when missing, writes their IDs into .env.local, and added a package.json script for rerunning setup safely.

tasks.title: Implement POST /api/email via Email Agent
tasks.description:
Create web/app/api/email/route.ts. Use the Managed Agents pattern:
1. Read EMAIL_AGENT_ID from process.env.
2. Create a session: anthropic.beta.agents.sessions.create({ agent_id: EMAIL_AGENT_ID }).
3. Send JSON.stringify({ items, summary }) as the user message, where items: ConsultItem[] and summary: SummaryData come from the request body.
4. Poll/stream until session completes and extract the assistant response text.
5. JSON.parse into { subject: string, body: string, tone: string }.
6. Return the parsed object.
Handle missing agent ID (500) and JSON parse errors (422).
Beta header required: 'managed-agents-2026-04-01'.
tasks.output: Added the email API route backed by the managed email agent, validating items/summary input, streaming the agent response, and returning parsed subject/body/tone JSON with route-level error handling.

tasks.title: Wire mock responses into all three AI API routes
tasks.description:
web/lib/mocks.ts is already written (MOCK_ITEMS, MOCK_STATS, MOCK_SUMMARY, MOCK_EMAIL). Codex only needs to wire it into the three routes.

In each route, add a mock guard at the top of the handler:
  if (process.env.MOCK_AI === 'true') { return NextResponse.json(<mock>) }

- web/app/api/extract/route.ts: return NextResponse.json({ items: MOCK_ITEMS, stats: MOCK_STATS })
- web/app/api/summarize/route.ts: return NextResponse.json(MOCK_SUMMARY)
- web/app/api/email/route.ts: return NextResponse.json(MOCK_EMAIL)

Also add MOCK_AI=true to web/.env.local.
tasks.output: Added MOCK_AI guards to the extract, summarize, and email API routes so local development can short-circuit to the existing mock payloads, and enabled MOCK_AI=true in web/.env.local.

tasks.title: Auto-parse on tab switch with per-tab sample content
tasks.description:
Two changes to web/app/page.tsx:

1. Populate initial content for all three tabs in initialSourceText:

   Email tab sample:
   'From: Sarah Chen <s.chen@acmecorp.com>
   To: Consulting team
   Subject: RE: Commercial strategy workstream — quick update

   Hi team, following up on our call. A few things:

   Legal has flagged the vendor contract renewal — we need sign-off from procurement before we can proceed. Targeting end of next week but this is at risk.

   Can someone own the competitive benchmarking deck? We promised the board a first draft by May 2.

   Also waiting on Marcus to confirm the go-live date for the APAC rollout. He was supposed to revert by yesterday.'

   Notes tab sample:
   'Whiteboard session — Apr 23

   Three priority areas agreed:
   - Pricing model needs rework before board presentation (owner: TBD, due May 5)
   - NPS data from Q2 still missing — blocked on analytics team access
   - Draft exec summary for CFO review by Apr 28 — JS taking point

   Open question: do we expand scope to include LATAM? Client leaning yes but no budget confirmed.'

2. Auto-parse on tab switch: in handleTabChange, after setting activeTab, check if the new tab has content (sourceText[tab].trim().length > 0). If yes, call handleParse immediately (using the new tab's content). This means switching tabs with content automatically triggers extraction and updates the board.

IMPORTANT: handleTabChange must read the new tab's source text — not the stale activeTab value. Pass the new tab value directly to the fetch body rather than relying on the activeTab state which won't have updated yet. Extract a shared parseFn(tab: SourceTab) helper that takes a tab argument instead of reading activeTab from closure.
tasks.output: Populated the Email and Notes tabs with sample source content, refactored parsing into a tab-aware helper, and made tab switches auto-trigger extraction using the newly selected tab's text instead of stale active state.

tasks.title: Make sidebar retractable
tasks.description:
Add collapse/expand behaviour to the sidebar.

1. In page.tsx: add sidebarOpen: boolean state (default true). Pass it and a toggle handler to Sidebar as props. Change the grid-cols from fixed `[232px_1fr]` to `[var(--sidebar-w)_1fr]` where --sidebar-w is set inline as a CSSProperty: 232px when open, 48px when collapsed.

2. In Sidebar.tsx:
   - Accept props: { collapsed: boolean; onToggle: () => void }
   - When collapsed (48px wide): hide all text labels, section headings, and engagement names. Show only icon columns — brand mark, nav icons, engagement color pips, user avatar. Center everything.
   - Add a toggle button at the top of the sidebar (chevron icon, rotates 180° when collapsed). Position it at the top-right edge.
   - Use `transition-all duration-200` on the sidebar root so the width animates smoothly.
   - When collapsed, show a tooltip (title attribute) on each nav icon so users still know what each item is.

3. The toggle button can use a simple ‹ / › or chevron SVG inline — no icon library needed.
tasks.output: Implemented a retractable sidebar driven by the page-level --sidebar-w CSS variable, added a top-right chevron toggle, and switched Sidebar to an icon-only layout with tooltips when collapsed. Verified the Next.js build succeeds.

tasks.title: Implement digest list and manual Generate digest flow
tasks.description:
Replace the auto-summarize-on-parse flow with a manual curator-driven digest.

## State changes (page.tsx)
- Add `digestIds: string[]` state (default `[]`) — ordered list of item IDs the user has pinned to the digest.
- Remove the automatic `generateSummary(payload.items)` call that fires after extraction. Summary starts null until the user triggers it.
- Add `handleAddToDigest(id: string)`: toggles the id in digestIds (add if absent, remove if present).
- Add `handleGenerateDigest()`: calls POST /api/summarize with `{ items: digestIds.map(id => items.find(...)) }` — only the pinned items, not the full list. Sets summary state on success.

## RightPanel changes
- Summary mode (selectedItem=null):
  - When digestIds is empty: show an empty state — 'No items in digest yet. Open a card and click Add to digest to get started.'
  - Show a 'Generate digest' primary button below the digest list. Disabled when digestIds is empty or isRegenerating. Active (accent colored) when digestIds.length > 0 and summary is null.
  - Show pinned item count pill next to the Generate button: e.g. '3 items'.
  - After summary is generated, show exec summary + client digest as before. Keep the Generate button visible so user can regenerate after adding/removing items.
- Detail mode (selectedItem set):
  - 'Add to digest' button: accent-colored when item is NOT in digestIds, muted/outlined when it IS (acts as toggle). Label switches: 'Add to digest' → 'In digest ✓'.

## Props changes
- RightPanel: add `digestIds: string[]`, `onAddToDigest: (id: string) => void`, `onGenerateDigest: () => void`.
- Remove `onRegenerate` prop (replaced by `onGenerateDigest`).
tasks.output: Replaced auto-summarize with a curator-driven digest: users can pin items from detail view, see a digest list in the RightPanel, and manually generate/regenerate a digest summary using only pinned items. Summary now stays null until generated.
